---
title: "Research"
# description: "Research interests and current work"
---

My research lies at the intersection of **household finance** and **behavioural finance**. I study how investors make decisions under uncertainty and ambiguity and how information is incorporated into prices.

## Research interests

- Household finance and retail investor behaviour
- Decision-making under ambiguity and uncertainty
- Belief formation, probability weighting, and investor attention
<!-- - Empirical asset pricing and information processing -->

## Working papers

### Information processing under ambiguity in financial markets

**joint work with Steffen Meyer and Charline Uhr**  
*Working paper, June 2026*

We show that ambiguity affects how information is incorporated into prices. Using
earnings announcements of 624 S&P 500 firms from 2006 to 2024, we find underreac-
tion to positive news and overreaction to negative news under high ambiguity, with
price discounts of 8% to 35%. These effects are not driven by expected volatility and
are robust to alternative ambiguity measures, highlighting a distinction between am-
biguity and risk. We further show that these effects persist for at least 10 days but
reverse within 30 days. This pattern suggests that ambiguity induces temporary price
distortions rather than persistent post-earnings announcement drift.

*Presented at: FMARC 2026 (received Doctoral Paper Runner-Up Award), DGF Annual Meeting 2025, 10th SDU Finance Workshop*

::: {.paper-links}
[Download paper (PDF)](files/papers/2025_ambiea.pdf){.button}
<!-- [Online appendix](files/papers/paper-name-appendix.pdf) -->
<!-- [Slides](files/papers/paper-name-slides.pdf) -->
<!-- [Code](https://github.com/lukasgrahl/repository-name) -->
:::


<!--
HOW TO ADD A PAPER

1. Save the PDF in files/papers/. A short, lower-case filename is easiest,
   for example: files/papers/ambiguity-retail-trading.pdf
2. Copy the block below outside this comment.
3. Replace the title, authors, status, description, and links.
4. Delete any links you do not need.

### Paper title

**With Coauthor Name**  
*Working paper, June 2026*

Two or three sentences describing the research question, method, and main contribution.

::: {.paper-links}
[Download paper (PDF)](files/papers/paper-name.pdf){.button}
[Online appendix](files/papers/paper-name-appendix.pdf)
[Slides](files/papers/paper-name-slides.pdf)
[Code](https://github.com/lukasgrahl/repository-name)
:::

---
-->

<!-- ## Work in progress -->

<!-- A selection of current projects examines revealed preferences from household portfolios, retail trading around earnings announcements, and the empirical distinction between distorted beliefs and non-standard preferences. -->
